meta.title: AutoCAD Interview Questions | Teks Academy
meta.description: Explore top AutoCAD interview questions and answers covering 2D drafting, 3D modeling, architectural design, engineering drawings, and CAD tools.

heading: AutoCAD
Interview Questions
heroSection.subHeading: Prepare for AutoCAD interviews with architecture and engineering-focused technical questions and answers.

questions.question: What is AutoCAD?
questions.answer: AutoCAD is a computer-aided design (CAD) software application used for creating 2D and 3D designs and drafting. It was developed by Autodesk.

questions.question: What file format does AutoCAD use?
questions.answer: DWG (Drawing) is the native file format used by AutoCAD.

questions.question: What is the use of the UCS in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: UCS stands for User Coordinate System. It allows users to define a custom coordinate system for drawing in 2D or 3D.

questions.question: What are layers in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Layers are like transparent overlays that help organize different elements of a drawing. Each layer can have its own color, linetype, and properties.

questions.question: How do you draw a line in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Use the LINE command, then specify start and end points.

questions.question: What is the difference between MODEL space and LAYOUT space?
questions.answer: MODEL space is where the actual drawing is created. LAYOUT space is used for plotting or printing and typically includes title blocks and viewports.

questions.question: What is a block in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: A block is a collection of objects grouped into a single object. It is useful for repeating content like doors or symbols.

questions.question: How do you insert a block?
questions.answer: Use the INSERT command or drag and drop from the tool palette or block library.

questions.question: What command is used to create circles?
questions.answer: The CIRCLE command.

questions.question: What is the function of the TRIM command?
questions.answer: It removes or trims objects that intersect or touch another object.

questions.question: What is the difference between ‘COPY’ and ‘MIRROR’?
questions.answer: COPY duplicates an object, while MIRROR creates a mirrored version about a specified axis.

questions.question: What is the purpose of object snap (OSNAP)?
questions.answer: OSNAP helps accurately select specific points on objects, like endpoints, midpoints, or intersections.

questions.question: How do you scale an object in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Use the SCALE command, select the object, then specify a base point and scale factor.

questions.question: What is the difference between absolute, relative, and polar coordinates?
questions.answer:
* Absolute: uses the origin (0,0)
* Relative: uses the last point as reference
* Polar: uses distance and angle from the last point

questions.question: How do you create a hatch in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Use the HATCH command and choose an area and pattern.

questions.question: What does the ARRAY command do?
questions.answer: It creates multiple copies of objects in a pattern — rectangular, polar, or path.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the ‘XREF’ command?
questions.answer: XREF (external reference) links an external drawing into your current drawing.

questions.question: What is a viewport?
questions.answer: A viewport displays a view of your model space drawing in a layout.

questions.question: What are dimension styles?
questions.answer: They control the appearance and formatting of dimensions.

questions.question: How do you match properties from one object to another?
questions.answer: Use the MATCHPROP or MATCH command.

questions.question: How can you clean up a drawing?
questions.answer: Use PURGE to remove unused items and OVERKILL to delete duplicate or overlapping objects.

questions.question: What is the difference between MODEL and PAPER space?
questions.answer: Model space is for designing; paper space is for printing and presentation.

questions.question: What is a polyline?
questions.answer: A polyline is a connected sequence of line or arc segments that is treated as a single object.

questions.question: How do you convert lines to a polyline?
questions.answer: Use the PEDIT command, then choose JOIN.

questions.question: What does the FILLET command do?
questions.answer: It creates a rounded corner between two lines.

questions.question: What is dynamic block in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: A dynamic block contains rules and constraints that allow you to change its appearance using grips or parameters.

questions.question: What are annotative objects?
questions.answer: Annotative objects automatically scale according to the viewport scale, such as text or dimensions.

questions.question: What is the use of the WIPEOUT command?
questions.answer: It creates a blank area to mask parts of the drawing under it.

questions.question: What is the difference between ‘BYLAYER’ and ‘BYBLOCK’?
questions.answer: BYLAYER uses the properties of the layer, while BYBLOCK inherits properties from the block until it's inserted.

questions.question: How do you import Excel data into AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Use DATAEXTRACTION or insert as an OLE object or linked table.

questions.question: What is the difference between GROUP and BLOCK?
questions.answer: GROUP allows easy selection of objects but can be ungrouped; BLOCK is a more permanent combination.

questions.question: How do you create custom linetypes?
questions.answer: By editing the LIN file or using MKLTYPE command.

questions.question: What is the difference between TEXT and MTEXT?
questions.answer: TEXT is single-line text, while MTEXT is multiline with formatting options.

questions.question: How can you automate repetitive tasks in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Using scripts, LISP routines, or AutoCAD macros.

questions.question: What is a plot style in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: A plot style controls how objects are printed, such as color, lineweight, and screening.

questions.question: What are constraints in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Constraints are used to control the geometry of objects, such as locking lengths, angles, or positions.

questions.question: What is the OVERKILL command?
questions.answer: It deletes overlapping or duplicate objects to clean the drawing.

questions.question: What is a CTB file?
questions.answer: A Color-dependent Plot Style Table file that maps colors to lineweights when plotting.

questions.question: How do you create a title block?
questions.answer: Design the layout, insert company logo, drawing fields, and save it as a block or template.

questions.question: What is the use of BATTMAN?
questions.answer: It manages attributes in a block.

questions.question: What is the difference between EXTEND and LENGTHEN?
questions.answer: EXTEND stretches to meet another object, LENGTHEN modifies length/angle directly.

questions.question: What are isometric drawings?
questions.answer: Isometric drawings represent 3D objects in 2D with 30-degree angles, useful in mechanical design.

questions.question: What is the function of ISOPLANE?
questions.answer: It switches between isometric planes for isometric drawing.

questions.question: How do you recover a corrupt drawing file?
questions.answer: Use RECOVER or AUDIT commands.

questions.question: How do you create 3D objects in AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Use 3D commands like EXTRUDE, REVOLVE, SWEEP, and PRESSPULL.

questions.question: What is a section plane?
questions.answer: It creates a cross-sectional view of a 3D model.

questions.question: What are solid, surface, and wireframe modeling?
questions.answer:
* Different methods of 3D modeling:
  - Solid: full volume
  - Surface: only outer faces
  - Wireframe: skeleton model

questions.question: What is the use of REGEN?
questions.answer: Regenerates the drawing and refreshes the display, useful after major changes.

questions.question: How do you customize the AutoCAD interface?
questions.answer: Using the CUI (Customize User Interface) dialog box.

questions.question: How do you apply material and lighting in 3D AutoCAD?
questions.answer: Use the MATERIALS and LIGHTING panels, and render the model for visualization.
